---
title: "HPC setup and SPEI Tiled Pipeline Guide (Matrix Method)"
author: "Eli R. Bendall, Hawkesbury Institute for the Environment, Western Sydney University"
funding: "This work was supported by Western Sydney University and with funding from the Australian Government under the National Environmental Science Program's Resilient Landscapes Hub"
orcid: "0000-0002-9120-3457"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
note: "Code developed with AI assistance from Claude (Anthropic), https://claude.ai"
output: html_document
---

# Overview

This guide walks through everything needed to access NCI Gadi, set up the
R environment, and submit the SPEI pipeline as a batch job. Run each chunk
in order where R code is provided. Shell commands (bash) should be run in
Terminal on your Mac or in the Gadi terminal after SSH login.

The ANUClimate v2.0 data is **already on Gadi** at
`/g/data/gh70/ANUClimate/v2-0/stable/month/` — no data upload required.

---

# Part 1 — NCI Account Setup

## Step 1.1 — Complete NCI Registration

If not already done, complete registration at:
**<https://my.nci.org.au/mancini/signup/0>**

During registration:

- Use your WSU institutional email address
- Enter the project code provided by your colleague
- Leave the software access field blank
- Agree to the conditions of use

Account approval typically takes **1–2 business days**. You will receive a
confirmation email with your NCI username (usually initials + numbers,
e.g. `ab1234`).

---

## Step 1.2 — Request Access to the ANUClimate Data Project

The ANUClimate data lives in NCI project `gh70`. This must be requested
separately from your compute project.

**Steps:**

1. Log into <https://my.nci.org.au>
2. Click **Projects** in the top menu
3. Search for `gh70`
4. Click **Request Membership**
5. Add a brief justification, e.g.:
   *"I need access to ANUClimate v2.0 monthly climate data for SPEI
   calculation as part of an ecological drought analysis project."*

Access is usually granted within **1 business day**.

---

## Step 1.3 — Verify Project Memberships

Once both approvals arrive, log into <https://my.nci.org.au> and confirm
you are a member of:

- Your WSU compute project (e.g. `ab12`) — provides CPU time and storage
- `gh70` — provides access to ANUClimate data

---

# Part 2 — Connecting to Gadi

## Step 2.1 — SSH into Gadi

Open Terminal on your Mac and connect:

```bash
ssh YOUR_USERNAME@gadi.nci.org.au
```

Replace `YOUR_USERNAME` with your NCI username (from the approval email).
You will be prompted for your NCI password.

After login you will see a prompt like:
```
[ab1234@gadi-login-07 ~]$
```

---

## Step 2.2 — Understand the Gadi Filesystem

Gadi has three key storage locations:

| Path | Purpose | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `/home/YOUR_USERNAME/` | Home directory | 10GB limit — scripts and configs only |
| `/scratch/YOUR_PROJECT/` | Fast scratch | Large temporary files — **deleted after 90 days** |
| `/g/data/YOUR_PROJECT/` | Persistent storage | Outputs and data to keep long-term |
| `/g/data/gh70/` | ANUClimate data | Read-only — already there, no download needed |

**All pipeline outputs should go to `/g/data/YOUR_PROJECT/`.**

---

## Step 2.3 — Create Working Directories

Run these commands on Gadi after SSH login:

```bash
# Replace YOUR_PROJECT with your actual project code
PROJECT=YOUR_PROJECT

# Create output and scratch directories
mkdir -p /g/data/$PROJECT/spei_outputs
mkdir -p /scratch/$PROJECT/spei_temp

# Create a directory for your scripts
mkdir -p ~/spei
cd ~/spei

echo "Directories created successfully"
```

---

# Part 3 — Install Required R Packages

R packages are installed once to your home directory and persist across
sessions. This only needs to be done once.

## Step 3.1 — Load R Module and Install Packages

On Gadi (after SSH login):

```bash
# Load R — always specify version
module load R/4.3.1

# Install required packages
R --vanilla -e "
install.packages(
  c('terra', 'SPEI', 'lubridate'),
  repos = 'https://mirror.aarnet.edu.au/pub/CRAN/',
  lib   = Sys.getenv('R_LIBS_USER')
)
"
```

If prompted to create a personal library directory, type `y`.

---

## Step 3.2 — Verify Packages Installed Correctly

```bash
R --vanilla -e "
library(terra)
library(SPEI)
library(lubridate)
cat('All packages loaded successfully\n')
cat('terra version:', as.character(packageVersion('terra')), '\n')
cat('SPEI version:',  as.character(packageVersion('SPEI')),  '\n')
"
```

If any package fails to load, re-run the install command from Step 3.1.

---

## Step 3.3 — Verify ANUClimate Data is Accessible

```bash
# Check gh70 data is readable
ls /g/data/gh70/ANUClimate/v2-0/stable/month/

# Should show: evap  frst  pw  rain  srad  tavg  tmax  tmin  vp  vpd

# Check a specific file
ls /g/data/gh70/ANUClimate/v2-0/stable/month/rain/1980/ | head -5
```

If you get "Permission denied", your `gh70` membership has not yet been
approved — wait and try again later.

---

# Part 4 — Transfer Pipeline Files to Gadi

## Step 4.1 — Update Project Code in Pipeline Files

Before transferring, open both `02_spei_tile_hpc.R` and `spei_submit.sh`
on your Mac and replace **all instances** of `YOUR_PROJECT` with your
actual NCI project code.

---

## Step 4.2 — Transfer Files to Gadi

Run this in **Terminal on your Mac** (not on Gadi):

```bash
# Replace YOUR_USERNAME and YOUR_PROJECT with your actual values
scp 02_spei_tile_hpc.R  02_spei_merge_hpc.R  spei_tile_job.sh  spei_merge_job.sh  spei_submit.sh YOUR_USERNAME@gadi.nci.org.au:~/spei/
```

---

## Step 4.3 — Verify Files Transferred

Back on Gadi:

```bash
ls ~/spei/
# Should show: spei_pipeline_gadi.R  spei_job.sh
```

---

# Part 5 — Submit the Job

## Step 5.1 — Do a Quick Sanity Check First

Before submitting the full job, verify the pipeline script can at least
find the data and load packages:

```bash
cd ~/spei

# Run a quick interactive test — requests 10 minutes on a login node
module load R/4.3.1
R --vanilla -e "
library(terra)
library(SPEI)
library(lubridate)

# Check data is accessible
f <- '/g/data/gh70/ANUClimate/v2-0/stable/month/rain/1980/ANUClimate_v2-0_rain_monthly_198001.nc'
r <- terra::rast(f)
cat('Test file loaded OK\n')
cat('Extent:', as.character(terra::ext(r)), '\n')
cat('Resolution:', terra::res(r), '\n')
"
```

---

## Step 5.2 — Submit the PBS Job

```bash
cd ~/spei
qsub spei_job.sh
```

The output will show your job ID:
```
1234567.gadi-pbs
```

**Save this job ID** — you need it to monitor and manage the job.

---

## Step 5.3 — Monitor Job Status

```bash
# Check job status — Q = queued, R = running, F = finished
qstat -u $USER

# More detailed status for a specific job
qstat -f 1234567.gadi-pbs

# Watch the live log output while running
tail -f ~/spei/spei_pipeline.log

# Check your project's service unit usage
nci_account -P YOUR_PROJECT
```

---

## Step 5.4 — Check Output After Job Completes

```bash
# Check the log file for completion message and any errors
cat ~/spei/spei_pipeline.log

# Verify output files were created
ls /g/data/YOUR_PROJECT/spei_outputs/

# Check a sample TIF has valid data
module load R/4.3.1
R --vanilla -e "
library(terra)
r <- rast('/g/data/YOUR_PROJECT/spei_outputs/SPEI6_monthly_tifs/SPEI6_201001.tif')
print(terra::global(r, 'range', na.rm = TRUE))
vals <- terra::extract(r, data.frame(x = 150, y = -33))
cat('Value at Sydney (Jan 2010):', vals[1,2], '\n')
"
```

---

# Part 6 — Download Outputs to Your Mac

Once the job is complete, download the outputs from Gadi to your LaCie
drive. Run this in **Terminal on your Mac**:

```bash
# Download all SPEI outputs 
scp -r YOUR_USERNAME@gadi.nci.org.au:/g/data/YOUR_PROJECT/spei_outputs \
    "/path/to/local/spei_outputs" # Update to your local output directory
```

For large transfers, `rsync` is more robust than `scp` as it can resume
if interrupted:

```bash
rsync -avz --progress \
  YOUR_USERNAME@gadi.nci.org.au:/g/data/YOUR_PROJECT/spei_outputs/ \
  "/path/to/local/spei_outputs" # Update to your local output directory
```

---

# Part 7 — Troubleshooting

## Job fails immediately

Check the log file:
```bash
cat ~/spei/spei_pipeline.log
```

Common causes:
- Wrong project code in `spei_job.sh` → update and resubmit
- `gh70` access not yet approved → wait and resubmit
- R packages not installed → rerun Step 3.1

## Job runs but TIFs are empty

Check that `gh70` data files are readable and that the `land_mask` is
being applied correctly:
```bash
module load R/4.3.1
R --vanilla -e "
library(terra)
f <- '/g/data/gh70/ANUClimate/v2-0/stable/month/rain/1980/ANUClimate_v2-0_rain_monthly_198001.nc'
r <- terra::rast(f)
v <- terra::extract(r, data.frame(x = 150, y = -33))
cat('Rain value at Sydney Jan 1980:', v[1,2], '\n')
"
```

## Job killed before finishing

The checkpoint system means it is safe to resubmit — completed SPEI
scales will be skipped automatically. Just resubmit:
```bash
qsub spei_job.sh
```

## Out of service units

Contact your WSU research computing team or NCI helpdesk:
**help@nci.org.au**

---

# Quick Reference — Useful Gadi Commands

```bash
# Check job queue
qstat -u $USER

# Delete a job
qdel JOB_ID

# Check storage usage
lquota

# Check service unit balance
nci_account -P YOUR_PROJECT

# List loaded modules
module list

# Available R versions
module avail R
```

---

*Guide prepared for SPEI pipeline deployment on NCI Gadi.
ANUClimate v2.0 source data: https://doi.org/10.25914/60a10acd183a2*
